title: Coronavirus Modelling — Boost Developing World Capacity
summary: In developing countries, where the coronavirus pandemic is potentially at its most dangerous and costly, the authors call for governments to work with academic institutions to build and sustain computational modelling capacity.
body: Correspondence The latest from Belfer See more Computational models of the likely spread of the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus have been instrumental in guiding governments' strategies to limit disease transmission and control the current public-health crisis. In developing countries, where the pandemic is potentially at its most dangerous and costly, we call for governments to work with academic institutions to build and sustain modelling capacity.... Recommended citation Iychettira, Kaveri and Afreen Siddiqi. "Coronavirus Modelling — Boost Developing World Capacity." Nature , vol. 581. no. 384. (May 26, 2020).
